title: Advice for someone moving to Denver - Request

transcript.text: Hello, video request from Archero William.
He goes, video request moving to Denver.
What would be the best strategy to move out to Denver?
How much savings do you think is necessary?
What are the best budget-minded areas of town to live in?
Do you prefer Boulder to Denver?
Thanks.
Well, that's a really open-ended question.
You didn't provide me any information about yourself.
But I'll do the video request anyways, because that's what I like.
I do video requests.
So, moving to Denver.
What's the best strategy to move to Denver?
Okay.
What you should do is don't be a fucking pussy, Arturo.
Load the car yourself.
Don't hire somebody to do it.
Do it yourself, man.
You know, and if you have back problems, then just lift with your legs.
You'll burn some calories that way.
You'll get rid of that beer gut.
Okay?
Now, if the car fills up, go out and get a trailer.
A U-Haul trailer.
And put the rest of your stuff in that U-Haul trailer.
Now, if you have too much stuff, then rent a U-Haul truck until your car behind it.
And then you drive out here.
You might want to try to get a job first before you move out here.
If you're a totally, like, basic dude, like a kid, just drive your car out and all your stuff you have in your car.
Leave all your stuff at home with your parents or sell it.
Get rid of it.
Don't bring anything out with you.
Live in your car in Denver.
So, come out in the springtime because it's really cold in the winter.
You do not want to live here in the wintertime.
Trust me.
Unless you have some kick-ass setup in your car where you can have a heater pumping all the time.
Like some kind of generator that's outside in your rocket box or something.
Yeah.
And then rent an apartment.
Some of the cheap parts of town are going to be Commerce City.
Northeastern part of Denver.
Commerce City or Aurora type area.
But the best place in Denver to live is, like, some place like Golden, Western Denver.
Because you're close to the mountains.
And if you want to go skiing, you don't have to go through traffic.
If you want to go hiking or mountain biking, you don't have to go through traffic.
The mountains are right there.
Way better.
But I'm sure the rent is more.
But I think it's better to have a tiny little one-room apartment or be a roommate in some house near the mountains than have a bigger place out in bumfuck nowhere.
But it just depends on what you do, you know.
So, let's see here.
How much saving do you think is necessary?
It depends on how frugal you are.
If you're really good at eating just canned vegetables and fruit, you could come out with 500 bucks and park your car someplace.
And just get a job at a temp agency, which will keep you afloat until you get an actual full-time job.
And then you're going.
And you're flying.
So, you don't need that much savings if you're a strong, disciplined person who doesn't spend money.
What are the best budget-minded areas of town?
Oh, yeah.
Aurora and Commerce City.
Do you prefer Boulder to Denver?
Yes, I prefer Boulder to Denver.
So, growing up, I always looked at Boulder like it's the ghetto, like it's the third world.
You go down there and you see, like, raggedy, homeless-looking people stumbling around.
And the roads aren't as maintained.
And houses are dilapidated.
And it's this big city.
A lot of, it's a grid, kind of on a grid system.
So, it freaks Europeans out who are used to these narrow, twisty roads.
It's kind of boring.
But the mountains are right there.
Great view of the mountains.
And it's really spread out, too.
The traffic's not that bad in Denver compared to other places.
But Boulder's way better.
Boulder is more expensive for rent.
But, you know, if you, I don't know, if you budget yourself in other ways, I think you get more money.
I think the local Walmart opened up in Boulder and their starting pay was $13.75 an hour.
So, you know, the pay that you get does offset the extra living expenses.
But a lot of people don't like Boulder.
I think especially people from Colorado Springs.
I've heard people mention stuff, like, walking around, and I think we were at the Garden of the Gods or something,
And my mom and her brother and nephew and stuff, and, where are you from, Boulder?
And he's like, oh, my God, I pity you.

So, Boulder's kind of like an oasis.
Kind of like Austin is an oasis in Texas.

I think they're just jealous.
But, uh, I would never live in Boulder.
I would live somewhere, anywhere else in the world.
I mean, in Denver.
Never want to live in Denver.
My mom was thinking about moving to Denver.
I was like, what the fuck?
No way.
There's no way you move to Denver.
Are you crazy?
Because she's like, yeah, I could get a bigger house there.
It's like, what the fuck?
No.
Yeah, so, Boulder.
Boulder's where it's at, man.
Because it's, um, it's a town, you know.
I like towns.

It's just a town.
